headline: USA RARE EARTH GOES ALL-IN: $2.8B Serra Verde Deal Secures Only Non-Asian Heavy REE Supplier
snippet: USA Rare Earth agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde Group for approximately $2.8 billion in cash and stock, gaining the Pela Ema mine — the only producer outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale. A U.S. government-backed 15-year offtake covers 100% of Serra Verde's neodymium, praseodymium, dysprosium, and terbium production.

headline: GOLD SLIPS to $4,763 as US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Stall — Trump Won't Extend Truce Without a Deal
snippet: Spot gold fell more than 1% to $4,763/oz on April 21 as investors await a second round of US-Iran ceasefire negotiations, with Trump signaling he won't extend the truce without a deal and the Strait of Hormuz remaining closed. Despite the pullback, gold remains approximately $1,400 above year-ago levels.

headline: SILVER BREAKS $79 — Sixth Straight Supply Deficit and Chinese Solar Demand Sustain the Squeeze
snippet: Spot silver climbed above $79/oz as the market heads for its sixth consecutive annual supply deficit, projected at 46 million ounces in 2026, with Chinese industrial demand sustaining pressure on physical markets. Silver has pulled back sharply from January's $121/oz peak but the structural deficit thesis — now in its sixth year — remains fully intact.

headline: Spanish Mountain Locks $55M Wheaton Royalty Stream on B.C. Gold Project — 1.5% NSR on All Production
snippet: Spanish Mountain Gold secured a $55 million streaming deal with Wheaton Precious Metals, with Wheaton acquiring a 1.5% net smelter return royalty on gold and silver production from the British Columbia project. The deal provides critical development financing as B.C. gold projects attract premium streaming bids in the current $4,700+ gold environment.

headline: Hycroft Brings in Engineers to Evaluate Underground Access at Nevada High-Grade Silver Discovery
snippet: Hycroft Mining (NASDAQ: HYMC) engaged engineering firm RESPEC to evaluate underground mining methods for the Brimstone and Vortex high-grade silver zones in Nevada, including a potential exploration decline for underground drill access. An Initial Assessment with economics is expected in Q2 2026, potentially reframing the asset as a high-grade underground play rather than a bulk-tonnage open-pit operation.

headline: Newmont Reports Q1 2026 Tomorrow — Analysts See $2.07 EPS and $6.36B Revenue With Gold Above $4,700
snippet: Newmont (NYSE: NEM) drops Q1 2026 earnings after market close on April 23, with consensus projecting EPS of $2.07 — up 65.6% year-over-year — on revenue of $6.36 billion. The print is the first major test of how gold-major margins held up with spot averaging above $4,500/oz through the quarter, and will set the tone for the entire sector.

headline: Switzerland's Gold Exports Surged 30% in March as London Vaults Absorb Record Institutional Flows
snippet: Swiss gold exports climbed 30% month-on-month in March, with deliveries to the UK hitting their highest since December — a sign that professional investors continue accumulating through London's OTC market. The flow data reinforces the broad-based institutional demand thesis underpinning gold's sustained run above $4,500.

headline: HSBC Poaches JPMorgan's Precious Metals Sales Director as Banks Race to Expand Gold Desks
snippet: HSBC hired JPMorgan executive director James Willis to lead its global precious metals sales operation, with Willis expected to join in mid-2026. The hire signals that major banks see sustained institutional demand for gold and silver warranting significant front-office investment.

headline: Uranium HITS $86.90/lb — Up 33% Year-on-Year as AI Data Centers Drive Nuclear Power Investment
snippet: Uranium spot reached $86.90/lb on April 20, up 33% year-over-year, as tech giants signing contracts for small modular reactors continue reshaping long-term demand projections. NexGen Energy is set to break ground on the Rook I mine in Saskatchewan this summer, while Paladin's Langer Heinrich uranium mine in Namibia targets full production by June.

headline: GoldSeek SWOT: Gold-to-Silver Ratio Collapse From 105 Is Flashing a Historic Buy Signal for Silver Miners
snippet: Sprott's weekly Gold SWOT analysis highlights that the gold-to-silver ratio's sharp contraction from above 105 is a historically reliable precursor to silver miner outperformance, with the SIL ETF having broken above a key resistance zone. China imported over 790 tonnes of silver in early 2026 driven by solar manufacturing — structural demand that supports the thesis even if investment flows stay subdued.
